posts.title: The Four Noble Truths: A Map of the Path
posts.description: The Buddha's first teaching remains a quietly revolutionary diagnosis of human experience — and a practical map of the way out.
posts.author: Dhammics

posts.title: Walking the Eightfold Path
posts.description: The Eightfold Path is not eight steps taken in sequence, but eight facets of a life that is no longer at war with itself.
posts.author: Dhammics

posts.title: The Art of Mindful Breathing
posts.description: Ānāpānasati — mindfulness of the breath — is deceptively simple. A gentle practice that, over time, quietly rearranges everything.
posts.author: Dhammics

posts.title: Anicca: The Quiet Gift of Impermanence
posts.description: Everything that arises passes away. The Buddha returned to this observation so often that it deserves our own long, slow look.
posts.author: Dhammics

posts.title: Metta: The Practice of Boundless Friendliness
posts.description: Metta is not sentimentality. It is the steady training of a heart that can meet any being without closing.
posts.author: Dhammics

posts.title: Walking Meditation: Dhamma in Motion
posts.description: Walking meditation is the quiet cousin of sitting. For many practitioners, it is where insight first lands.
posts.author: Dhammics

posts.title: The Five Precepts as Training, Not Commandments
posts.description: The precepts are not a moral code imposed from above. They are a training — a laboratory for seeing what the mind does when the door is closed to certain habits.
posts.author: Dhammics

posts.title: Dependent Origination: How Suffering Assembles Itself
posts.description: Paticca-samuppada, the Buddha's teaching of interdependent arising, is often presented as a dense twelve-link formula. At its heart is a simple, liberating insight.
posts.author: Dhammics
